HomeDocs

Tables with rowspan and colspan

This article exercises the matrix-IR table renderer against cases the stock GFM plugin mishandles: a header row that uses both colspan (to group two sub-columns) and rowspan (to lift a column above a sub-header), followed by a headerless data table.

Grouped header with spans

The first table groups two identity columns under a Person banner via colspan=2, and lifts Notes above the sub-header via rowspan=2. The body rows then carry CSV-quotable text (an embedded comma and a double-quote) so the renderer is exercised on both axes at once.

PersonNotes
NameAge
Alice30hello, world
Bob25"quoted"

Headerless data table

A plain two-column data table with no thead: every row is a td row, so the stock GFM plugin keeps it as raw HTML. Under the tables option, row 0 is treated as the header and the rest become data.

Apple$1.00
Banana$0.50

Why this matters

Readability preserves rowspan/colspan on the cloned article DOM, so the matrix IR walks the source spans directly rather than trusting turndown's pre-computed cell text. Resolving them once into a dense grid means CSV, JSON, and GFM all render from the same structure.

Related
